examples: It is impossible to make up the lost sleeping hours during the workweek.
During the 1920s, most advocates of scientific management, Frederick Taylor's method for maximizing workers' productivity by rigorously routinizing their jobs, opposed the five-day workweek.
The average workweek was unchanged at 34.2 hours.
Instead of the usual five-day workweek, employees work four ten-hour shifts per week, with no overtime.
At Navsys, five of its 20 remaining workers have shifted to a four-day workweek.
